description: Replace the existing JSON-based project export/import with a ZIP-based format that bundles images as files inside the archive. Uses JSZip library. Modifies exportSingleProject, exportAllData, handleImport, and importAllData in App.jsx.
steps: Run 'npm install jszip' to add JSZip as a dependency in package.json

Create a new service file src/zipExportImportService.js that encapsulates all ZIP export/import logic. This keeps the already-large App.jsx from growing further. The service should export two main functions: exportProjectAsZip(projectData, projectName) and importProjectFromZip(file, uploadImageFn). Details below.

exportProjectAsZip(projectData, projectName): (1) Deep-clone projectData. (2) Collect all image objects from all workspaces that have a 'url' property (Firebase URLs). (3) Fetch each image URL as a blob. (4) Generate a filename for each image: use pattern '<imageId>.<extension>' where extension is derived from the blob's content-type (default to 'jpg'). (5) In the cloned projectData, replace each image's 'url' property with a 'filename' property set to the generated filename. Remove 'src' field if present. (6) Create a JSZip instance. Add 'project.json' containing JSON.stringify of the modified projectData. Add each image blob to 'images/<filename>'. (7) Generate the zip blob and trigger browser download as '<projectName>.zip'.

importProjectFromZip(zipFile, uploadImageFn): (1) Load the ZIP file with JSZip. (2) Extract and parse 'project.json'. (3) List all files under 'images/' folder. (4) For each workspace in the project, iterate its images array. For each image that has a 'filename' property (not 'url'), find the corresponding file in the ZIP's images/ folder. (5) Upload the image blob to Firebase Storage using the provided uploadImageFn(blob, projectId, workspaceId, imageId) which returns a download URL. (6) Replace the 'filename' property with 'url' set to the new download URL. (7) Return the fully restored project data object.
In src/App.jsx, import the two new functions from zipExportImportService.js.
Modify exportSingleProject in App.jsx: instead of creating a JSON blob and downloading it, call exportProjectAsZip(exportData, target.name). The function is already async so this is straightforward.

Modify exportAllData in App.jsx: wrap the backup object (containing all projects) in a ZIP using the same pattern - collect images from all projects' workspaces, download blobs, replace URLs with filenames, bundle into a single ZIP named 'thoughtflow-backup-<date>.zip'.

Modify handleImport in App.jsx: detect whether the file is a .zip or .json. If .zip, use importProjectFromZip passing uploadImage from imageStorageService as the upload function, then restore state from the returned project data. If .json, keep existing behavior.

Modify importAllData in App.jsx: similarly detect .zip vs .json. For .zip, extract and import using the new service. The ZIP for full backup will contain a project.json with the backup wrapper format ({type, version, projects}). Images from all projects go into a single images/ folder with unique filenames.
Update the file input elements to accept '.zip,.json' instead of just '.json' so users can select ZIP files.
Run 'npm run build' to verify no compilation errors.
acceptance_criteria: JSZip is listed as a dependency in package.json
src/zipExportImportService.js exists with exportProjectAsZip and importProjectFromZip functions
exportSingleProject produces a .zip file containing project.json and images/ folder
exportAllData produces a .zip file containing project.json (backup format) and images/ folder
handleImport and importAllData accept both .zip and .json files
When importing a .zip, images are uploaded to Firebase Storage and URLs are restored in the project data
project.json inside the ZIP uses 'filename' property on images instead of 'url'
verification: Run 'npm run build' and confirm it exits with code 0
